diagrams.title: If-Else Decision Flow
diagrams.description: A flowchart showing the execution path of if, else if, and else statements in C++, demonstrating how the program evaluates conditions sequentially and executes the first true condition's block.
diagrams.purpose: Explains how nested decision making works in C++ and the importance of condition order

diagrams.title: C++ Loop Types Comparison
diagrams.description: A side-by-side visual comparison of for, while, and do-while loops in C++, showing their structure, syntax placement of initialization, condition, and increment/decrement.
diagrams.purpose: Helps learners understand when to use each loop type and the key differences in their execution patterns

diagrams.title: Switch-Case Flow Diagram
diagrams.description: A flowchart illustrating the switch statement execution in C++, showing how a value is compared against multiple cases and the importance of break statements to prevent fall-through.
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates switch statement mechanics and explains the break statement's role in controlling flow
